title: Potent Umami Russian Beef Stew
description: A rich and savory one-pot Russian dish featuring tender beef and a depth of umami flavor, achieved through the use of mushrooms, soy sauce, and beef broth, all slow-cooked to perfection using sous-vide techniques.
instructions: Preheat the sous-vide water bath to 130 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the beef short ribs with salt, black pepper, and dried thyme.
Sear the beef short ribs in a hot skillet until browned on all sides.
Add the sliced mushrooms, chopped onion, and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the soy sauce, beef broth, and tomato paste to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a large sous-vide bag or a heavy-duty zip-top plastic bag.
Seal the bag and place it in the preheated water bath.
Cook the stew for 48 hours, or until the beef is tender and falling off the bone.
Remove the bag from the water bath and let it cool to room temperature.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan, discarding the solids.
Bring the sauce to a simmer over medium heat and cook until it has thickened slightly.
